data.title: KŌZĒ Stays adds $10M+ Mont-Tremblant Estate to its portfolio, solidifying dominance in luxury rental market.
data.description: KŌZĒ Stays, a prominent vacation rental management company in the Mont-Tremblant and Laurentians area, has recently included a prestigious private estate in their collection. Named the Exclusive Tremblant Cochrane Luxury Estate, this property, valued at over $10,000,000, is now open for rentals through KŌZĒ Stays. Setting a new standard in the region, it stands out as one of the largest private residences available for rent in Mont-Tremblant.

data.title: Dogecoin and Shiba Inu in symmetrical triangle patterns, suggesting 10-15% moves, with Dogecoin possibly bottoming at $0.083. Dogecoin's Supertrend at $0.10378 remains bearish. Open interest for Dogecoin increased by 2.30% to $1.08.
data.description: Dogecoin (DOGE) and Shiba Inu (SHIB) are currently experiencing stable trading patterns, as both meme coins are confined within symmetrical triangles, indicating potential imminent movements of 10-15% in either direction. A notable development is Dogecoin's Coiled Spring and its Parabolic SAR flipping below the price point of $0.09562, the first positive signal in weeks which implies that the recent low of $0.083 may represent a bottom. Despite this, the Supertrend at $0.10378 remains bearish, causing conflicting signals between indicators. The coin is finding support from an ascending trendline starting at $0.083, while facing resistance from a descending trendline originating from January highs which limits price increases to around $0.115-$0.12. This has resulted in a wedge formation compressing the price between $0.095-$0.105. Notably, derivatives data indicates a growing level of confidence, with open interest escalating by 2.30% to $1.08 billion. For a comprehensive account, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Following Supreme Court defeat, Trump aims to enforce worldwide tariffs using alternative laws. Administration readied for this, with months of planning.
data.description: For several months, government officials have been getting ready in case the court rules against the president, creating backup strategies.

data.title: Bitcoin's sell pressure decreasing as whales depositing on exchanges to sell; BTC down 46% from October peak.
data.description: The value of Bitcoin has decreased by 46% since its peak in October, with the largest holders continuing to move their coins to exchanges, potentially indicating intentions to sell. This data comes from CryptoQuant, a crypto analytics platform.

data.title: Federal budget at risk as tariffs face uncertainty after Supreme Court decision. Trump may replace $X revenue with new levies.
data.description: The tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court had been a significant source of revenue. President Trump stated he could compensate for the lost funds by imposing alternative taxes.

data.title: MIC Global expands in GCC from Doha, secures Qatar approval for MiIncome, strengthens partnership with QIC. #insurance #expansion
data.description: In Doha, Qatar on February 20, 2026, MIC Global, a top embedded micro-insurance provider and Lloyd's Coverholder, revealed a significant expansion throughout the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region. The expansion will be centered in Doha and will be executed in collaboration with Qatar in a strategic partnership. This move signifies a major development for the company in the region.

data.title: Trump stands by tariffs despite Supreme Court ruling. US economy grew 1.4% in Q4, down from 4.4%. Inflation at 3%. SPY stock hits highs.
data.description: The US economy expanded at a 1.4% annualized pace in the fourth quarter, a significant slowdown from the prior quarter's 4.4% growth and below expectations of 3%. The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge rose to a 3% annual rate in December. President Trump attributed the weak growth to the government shutdown, estimating it reduced GDP by 2%. The Supreme Court made a landmark decision revoking the administration's tariff authority under the IEEPA, asserting that only Congress can impose tariffs. State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust stock is trading near recent highs. Trump also blamed tariffs for the growth slowdown.

data.title: Surge Copper's Berg Project accepted into BC Critical Minerals Office, Feb 20, 2026. TSXV: SURG, OTCQB: SRGXF, Frankfurt: G6D2.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 20, 2026, Surge Copper Corp. shared that the Berg Project has been accepted into the Critical Minerals Office by the British Columbia government. Surge Copper Corp. trades on the TSXV as SURG, on the OTCQB as SRGXF, and on the Frankfurt exchange as G6D2. This move signifies the official recognition of the project's importance in the critical minerals sector.

data.title: Century Aluminum Company response to US Supreme Court IEEPA decision on tariffs. (NASDAQ: CENX) issued a statement on Feb 20, 2026.
data.description: In Chicago on February 20, 2026, Century Aluminum Company (NASDAQ: CENX) released a statement concerning the recent U.S. Supreme Court ruling on tariffs enacted pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

data.title: FaceTec aims to prevent $30B+ healthcare identity fraud using UR® Codes to verify patient identities.
data.description: FaceTec introduces UR® Codes technology in SUMMERLIN, Nevada on Feb. 20, 2026. This innovation combines humans with their verified identities through various documents, ensuring robust patient matching and preventing entitlement fraud and deceitful service provision. FaceTec, known for its pioneering 3D Face Verification software, spearheads this development.

data.title: Matt Shaw reappointed as Vice President of Sales at Mighty Auto Parts, to drive sales strategy for product lines & revenue growth.
data.description: In Peachtree Corners, Georgia on February 20, 2026, Mighty Auto Parts made public that Matt Shaw has been reinstated as the Vice President of Sales. In this position, Shaw will spearhead sales strategy and implementation to drive the rapid growth of essential product lines and revenue within the Mighty system.

data.title: Graphic Packaging Holding Company will present at Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 4. CEO Robbert Rietbroek to speak. $GPK #packaging
data.description: Atlanta, GA - On February 20, 2026, Graphic Packaging Holding Company (NYSE: GPK) shared an update on their leadership team. Robbert Rietbroek, the President and Chief Executive Officer, along with Charles "Chuck" Lischer, the Senior Vice President, were mentioned in the announcement. Graphic Packaging is recognized as a key player in sustainable consumer packaging globally.
